# Generated by segmentation-advisor — pfSense inter-zone policy.
# Apply each block on the named interface (Firewall > Rules).
# pfSense blocks all traffic not matched by a pass rule, so no
# explicit default-deny entries are needed.

## Interface: DMZ  (10.10.10.0/24 — Public-facing web tier)
  [1] pass  proto tcp  from DMZ net  to 10.10.20.0/24  port 8080
      description: Web tier may call internal app APIs on observed ports only; PCI DSS v4.0 Req 1.2/1.3 — restrict traffic to that which is necessary

## Interface: APP_TIER  (10.10.20.0/24 — Internal application services)
  [1] pass  proto tcp  from APP_TIER net  to 10.10.30.0/24  port 3306
      description: Only the application tier may query databases, on observed DB ports; PCI DSS v4.0 scoping guidance — segmentation isolates the cardholder/sensitive data environment and reduces audit scope
  [2] pass  proto tcp  from APP_TIER net  to 10.10.30.0/24  port 5432
      description: Only the application tier may query databases, on observed DB ports; PCI DSS v4.0 scoping guidance — segmentation isolates the cardholder/sensitive data environment and reduces audit scope
  [3] pass  proto tcp  from APP_TIER net  to 10.10.60.0/24  port 53
      description: Domain clients need DNS/Kerberos/LDAP from domain controllers; SMB (445) deliberately excluded — NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 4 — no implicit trust from network location
  [4] pass  proto tcp  from APP_TIER net  to 10.10.60.0/24  port 88
      description: Domain clients need DNS/Kerberos/LDAP from domain controllers; SMB (445) deliberately excluded — NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 4 — no implicit trust from network location
  [5] pass  proto tcp  from APP_TIER net  to 10.10.60.0/24  port 389
      description: Domain clients need DNS/Kerberos/LDAP from domain controllers; SMB (445) deliberately excluded — NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 4 — no implicit trust from network location
  [6] pass  proto tcp  from APP_TIER net  to 10.10.60.0/24  port 636
      description: Domain clients need DNS/Kerberos/LDAP from domain controllers; SMB (445) deliberately excluded — NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 4 — no implicit trust from network location

## Interface: USER_VLAN  (10.10.40.0/24 — End-user workstations)
  [1] pass  proto tcp  from USER_VLAN net  to 10.10.10.0/24  port 80
      description: Users may reach published web services only; NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 3 — access granted per-session with least privilege
  [2] pass  proto tcp  from USER_VLAN net  to 10.10.10.0/24  port 443
      description: Users may reach published web services only; NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 3 — access granted per-session with least privilege
  [3] pass  proto tcp  from USER_VLAN net  to 10.10.20.0/24  port 8080
      description: Users may reach internal applications on observed ports; NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 3 — access granted per-session with least privilege
  [4] pass  proto tcp  from USER_VLAN net  to 10.10.60.0/24  port 53
      description: Domain clients need DNS/Kerberos/LDAP from domain controllers; SMB (445) deliberately excluded — NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 4 — no implicit trust from network location
  [5] pass  proto tcp  from USER_VLAN net  to 10.10.60.0/24  port 88
      description: Domain clients need DNS/Kerberos/LDAP from domain controllers; SMB (445) deliberately excluded — NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 4 — no implicit trust from network location
  [6] pass  proto tcp  from USER_VLAN net  to 10.10.60.0/24  port 389
      description: Domain clients need DNS/Kerberos/LDAP from domain controllers; SMB (445) deliberately excluded — NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 4 — no implicit trust from network location
  [7] pass  proto tcp  from USER_VLAN net  to 10.10.60.0/24  port 636
      description: Domain clients need DNS/Kerberos/LDAP from domain controllers; SMB (445) deliberately excluded — NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 4 — no implicit trust from network location
  [8] pass  proto tcp  from USER_VLAN net  to 10.10.50.0/24  port 631
      description: Workstations may print, nothing more; IoT stays isolated — NIST SP 800-207 §3.1.2 — network micro-segmentation
  [9] pass  proto tcp  from USER_VLAN net  to 10.10.50.0/24  port 9100
      description: Workstations may print, nothing more; IoT stays isolated — NIST SP 800-207 §3.1.2 — network micro-segmentation

## Interface: MANAGEMENT  (10.10.60.0/24 — Identity and administration)
  [1] pass  proto tcp  from MANAGEMENT net  to 10.10.10.0/24  port 22
      description: Administrative access (SSH/RDP) originates only from the management zone via the jump host; NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 3 — access granted per-session with least privilege
  [2] pass  proto tcp  from MANAGEMENT net  to 10.10.20.0/24  port 22
      description: Administrative access (SSH/RDP) originates only from the management zone via the jump host; NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 3 — access granted per-session with least privilege
  [3] pass  proto tcp  from MANAGEMENT net  to 10.10.30.0/24  port 22
      description: Administrative access (SSH/RDP) originates only from the management zone via the jump host; NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 3 — access granted per-session with least privilege
  [4] pass  proto tcp  from MANAGEMENT net  to 10.10.40.0/24  port 3389
      description: Administrative access (SSH/RDP) originates only from the management zone via the jump host; NIST SP 800-207 §2.1 Tenet 3 — access granted per-session with least privilege

# Implicit per-interface default: block all remaining traffic.
